</action>

<thought>
The agent is at the bottom center of the local view, facing north. The cell immediately in front (north) is floor ('.'), so moving forward is safe. The cells to the left and right are lava and wall, respectively, so turning is not beneficial. The goal is not yet visible, but moving forward will allow further exploration.
</thought>
<action>

</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing north (up) at the bottom-center of the view. Directly ahead is a wall (`#`), so moving forward is impossible. To the left (west) is lava (`L`), and to the right (east) is a wall (`#`). The goal (`G`) is not visible, indicating it likely lies in an unexplored area, possibly to the south behind the agent. The only safe immediate action is to reorient. I will turn right (clockwise) to eventually face south and move backward into unknown but potentially safe territory.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>

</thought>
<action>

<thought>

There is no goal visible yet. The only safe move is to step forward onto the floor cell to explore further. Moving forward will reveal more of the map and avoid the deadly lava.
</thought>
<action>

</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing north (up). Directly ahead is a wall (#), to the left is a wall (#), and to the right is lava (L). Moving forward would hit the wall, so it's not allowed. Turning left would face the wall, and turning right would face lava, but no movement is forced yet. To find a safe path, the agent should turn to face south and check the unknown area behind. Since turning right twice achieves that, the first step is to TURN_RIGHT.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

</thought>
<action>

<thought>
The agent is facing north (up). The cell directly ahead is a wall ('#'), so moving forward is impossible. To the left (west) is lava ('L'), and to the right (east) is also a wall ('#'). No safe forward move is available from the current orientation. The only unexplored direction is behind (south), which is not visible in the local view. To reach it, the agent must turn around. Turning right (clockwise) will first face east (wall), which is safe, and a subsequent right turn will face south. This sequence avoids facing deadly lava directly. Therefore, the first action should be to turn right.
